title: Promote the hosted control slab into the screen-core asset
summary: 0.4.12 gives the current screen-core player asset its own authored hosted control slab instead of leaving the hosted runtime dependent on the older external Meta layout. The exported CUA screen now carries a real local panel with scrub, volume, mute, skip, previous, play-pause, and next controls; the hosted runtime binds that screen-core panel to a repo-local control contract; and the control-surface state now includes hover so rollover can become a first-class visual seam instead of an implicit one.
reasoning: The runtime-side interaction, follow, drag, and player-binding seams were already solid. What kept the Meta-style hosted surface from becoming product was that the current live asset lane still exported only a bare screen core and the hosted runtime kept hiding the authored control surface. This slice promotes a minimal, self-contained control panel directly into the current CUA screen so the hosted player can finally use a local authored panel that matches the asset you actually deploy and test.

changes.summary: Extends the current `player-screen-2018` exporter so the versioned screen-core asset now authors a real hosted control slab beneath the screen and moves the `controls_anchor` onto that panel.
changes.reasoning: The current release lane needed a true authored surface in the shipped asset, not another proof-only control package or a hidden placeholder.

changes.summary: Makes direct screen-core CUA hosts bind against a local screen-core control contract with simple stable action ids instead of inheriting the older external Meta snapshot contract.
changes.reasoning: The new screen-core panel has its own authored layout, so the hosted runtime must target that local panel deterministically instead of relying on mismatched historical node maps.

changes.summary: Adds hover tracking to the local control-surface state model and exposes the hovered element in state payloads.
changes.reasoning: Interaction and update were already present; hover was the remaining missing state seam needed for complete hosted panel behavior and future rollover feedback.

changes.summary: Stops hiding the authored hosted control surface in the direct CUA runtime path so the live panel can actually render and receive interaction on the deployed asset.
changes.reasoning: The old placeholder behavior made sense before the panel was promoted, but it blocks the current authored panel from becoming a visible product surface.

verification: Build and deploy the versioned `fa_cua_player.0.4.12.dll` and `fa_player_asset.0.4.12.assetbundle` through the player foundation script.
Run player screen-core release validation for 0.4.12 and keep the generated receipt beside the release shelf.

In live VaM, attach the 0.4.12 screen-core asset to a `CustomUnityAsset`, confirm the hosted control slab renders beneath the screen, and verify play-pause, scrub, volume, mute, previous, next, and skip respond on the authored panel.
knownIssues: This first promoted panel is intentionally minimal and does not yet recreate the full legacy Meta video-player visual language inside the screen-core asset.
Hover is now tracked in the hosted control-surface state model, but this slice does not yet add dedicated rollover art per control element.
Shell-family-wide control-panel authoring and shell-specific layout polish remain separate follow-on work.
